notes: Lenalidomide is a potent inhibitor of TNF-alpha production, a core IMiD property. TNF role in keloids is context-dependent (pro-proliferative via NF-kB but also induces MMPs), so net effect is unclear.
Lenalidomide reduces VEGF expression in myeloma cell microvesicles, contributing to anti-angiogenic activity. Indirect effect via cereblon-mediated pathways.
Lenalidomide has antiangiogenic effects on endothelial cells and suppresses VEGF receptor signaling. Related IMiDs shown to affect KDR/VEGFR2 expression on endothelial progenitor cells.
Lenalidomide inhibits beta-catenin associations with cadherin-5 and CD31 in endothelial cells. Systems biology analysis identifies CTNNB1 as central IMiD-targeted transcription factor.
Indirect effect — rituximab depletes CD20+ B cells, which are major IL-6 producers. RA clinical studies demonstrate significant serum IL-6 reduction following rituximab treatment.
No direct or well-documented indirect effect on JAK2. Rituximab targets CD20 on B cells; any JAK2 modulation would be a distant downstream consequence of reduced cytokine milieu.
Indirect — B cells produce TGF-β1; depletion via rituximab may reduce TGF-β1 levels. Limited evidence from systemic sclerosis studies suggests reduced TGF-β signaling post-rituximab.
